task: dec.More() does not indicate whether additional top-level JSON values exist after the first Decode. For JSONL input like {...}\n{...}, dec.Decode(&parsed) will succeed on the first object and dec.More() will be false (it’s only meaningful when iterating inside an array/object), so this branch will incorrectly treat JSONL as a single JSON value and only collect/apply replacements from the first entry. Consider verifying end-of-input by consuming trailing whitespace and checking for EOF (e.g., attempt a second Decode and require io.EOF, or peek the next token) before taking the single-JSON fast path; also add a regression test with multiple JSON objects to ensure all lines are redacted.
user_feedback_text: dec.More() does not indicate whether additional top-level JSON values exist after the first Decode. For JSONL input like {...}\n{...}, dec.Decode(&parsed) will succeed on the first object and dec.More() will be false (it’s only meaningful when iterating inside an array/object), so this branch will incorrectly treat JSONL as a single JSON value and only collect/apply replacements from the first entry. Consider verifying end-of-input by consuming trailing whitespace and checking for EOF (e.g., attempt a second Decode and require io.EOF, or peek the next token) before taking the single-JSON fast path; also add a regression test with multiple JSON objects to ensure all lines are redacted.
